title: monardella exilis
intro: Monardella exilis, also known as Purple Mountain Monardella, is a rare and endangered flowering plant native to the Channel Islands, specifically on Santa Cruz Island, California. It belongs to the Lamiaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants, flavonoids, and terpenoids, making it beneficial for promoting cardiovascular health, reducing inflammation, and fighting against oxidative stress. Traditional herbal preparations include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support immune function and overall well-being.
